emergent_themes.description: Social anxiety around shared kitchen spaces directly triggers delivery ordering as an avoidance mechanism
Extreme focus on portable, quick-consumption food driven by academic workload and time pressure
Romantic partner acts as primary social enabler for delivery orders, providing both cost-sharing and emotional permission

coding_summary: P005 demonstrates highly functional, efficiency-driven eating patterns with delivery serving as both a social bonding tool (with brother/partner) and kitchen avoidance mechanism. Strong analytical mindset applied to cost-benefit analysis of ordering, with clear articulation of student-specific friction points around minimum orders. Diary reveals more frequent delivery usage than interview suggests, particularly in social contexts.

coverage_gaps.reason: Participant is current undergraduate with no post-graduation experience or concrete future plans discussed
